transcript: for a way for fairgoers to eat their meatballs on the go. Fletch Davis, the Menchez brothers, Charlie Negrin, and others have laid claim to the invention. However, the first established food cart to sell something named a hamburger sandwich was Louie's Lunch in New Haven, Connecticut in the year 1900. Probably the single most important moment in modern hamburger history was unquestionably the standardization of the hamburger sandwich.
